perturbation.original_text: 3. Marking . All information disclosed under this Agreement is deemed Confidential Information whether or not it is so marked. It is the intent of the Parties to mark information as confidential and/or proprietary prior to release to the Receiving Party. However, such markings may be overlooked and or disclosed verbally or visually and shall not diminish the value of its confidentiality.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Marking . Information disclosed under this Agreement is deemed Confidential Information only if it is conspicuously marked as 'Confidential.' It is the intent of the Parties to mark information as confidential and/or proprietary prior to release to the Receiving Party. However, such markings may be overlooked and or disclosed verbally or visually and shall not diminish the value of its confidentiality.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the marking requirement so that information must be 'conspicuously marked' as Confidential directly contradicts the existing text 'However, such markings may be overlooked and or disclosed verbally or visually and shall not diminish the value of its confidentiality.' It creates an in-text contradiction regarding whether unmarked information is protected.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Marking . Information disclosed under this Agreement is deemed Confidential Information only if it is conspicuously marked as 'Confidential.' It is the intent of the Parties to mark information as confidential and/or proprietary prior to release to the Receiving Party. However, such markings may be overlooked and or disclosed verbally or visually and shall not diminish the value of its confidentiality.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original requirement for confidential information. Originally, information was 'deemed Confidential Information whether or not it is so marked.' The changed text states that information is deemed Confidential Information 'only if it is conspicuously marked as 'Confidential.'' This creates a direct contradiction because the original text protects unmarked confidential information, while the changed text does not. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, emphasizing the in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Return of Confidential Information . All Confidential Information and any copies and extracts thereof shall be promptly returned to the disclosing party or at any time within thirty (30) days of receipt of a written request by the disclosing party for the return of such Confidential Information. If authorized by the disclosing party, such Confidential Information may be destroyed by the Receiving Party if such destruction is certified by the Receiving Party to the satisfaction of the disclosing party.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Return of Confidential Information . All Confidential Information shall become the property of the receiving party upon the contract ending. If authorized by the disclosing party, such Confidential Information may be destroyed by the Receiving Party if such destruction is certified by the Receiving Party to the satisfaction of the disclosing party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the return clause to state that confidential information becomes the property of the receiving party contradicts section 6b, which states 'Confidential Information shall be disclosed only to employees, agents, and other parties of the Receiving Party who have a “need to know” in connection with the purposes stated herein'. With the confidential information being the receiving party's property, the contradiction will create uncertainty about ownership and use after the contract terminates, especially in the event of legal action.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Limitation on Use and Disclosure of Confidential Information . Confidential Information shall be used solely for the purpose of sharing technical, business and financial information to foster potential mutually beneficial business relationships. (a) Confidential Information shall not be copied or reproduced by the Receiving Party, except for such copies as may be reasonably required for accomplishment of the purposes stated herein; (b) Confidential Information shall be disclosed only to employees, agents, and other parties of the Receiving Party who have a “need to know” in connection with the purposes stated herein; and (c) This Agreement shall not restrict the disclosure or use of information that: (i) was in the public domain at the time of disclosure or thereafter enters the public domain through no breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party; (ii) was, at the time of the receipt by the Receiving Party, otherwise known to the Receiving Party without restrictions as to the use or disclosure; (iii) becomes known to the Receiving Party from a source other than the disclosing party without breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party; or (iv) is developed independently by the Receiving Party and without reliance upon the Confidential Information disclosed herein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 4 states that confidential information becomes the property of the receiving party upon contract termination. This directly contradicts Section 6(b), which limits the disclosure of confidential information to only those employees, agents, and other parties of the Receiving Party who have a “need to know”. If the Receiving Party owns the confidential information, the 'need to know' limitation becomes irrelevant, creating a clear contradiction about the rights and obligations related to that information after the contract ends. The original text required the return of confidential information, maintaining consistent ownership, and aligning with the restriction on disclosure.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Arbitration and Equitable Relief. (a) Arbitration . Except as provided herein, the Parties agree that any dispute or controversy arising out of or relating to any interpretation, construction, performance or breach of this Agreement shall be settled by arbitration to be held in San Diego County, California, USA in accordance with the Commercial Arbitration Rules then in effect for the American Arbitration Association. The arbitrator may grant injunctions or other relief in such dispute or controversy. In the event of arbitration, the Parties may undertake a reasonable amount of discovery. The decision of the arbitrator shall be final, conclusive and binding on the parties to the arbitration. Judgement may be entered on the arbitrator’s decision in any court having jurisdiction. (b) Equitable Remedies . The Parties agree that it would be impossible or inadequate to measure and calculate damages from any breach of the covenants set forth herein. Accordingly, the Parties agree that if in the event of a breach of the covenants contained in this Agreement, the affected party will have available, in addition to any other right or remedy available, the right to obtain an injunction from a court of competent jurisdiction restraining such breach or threatened breach and to specify performance of any such provision of the Agreement. The Parties further agree that no bond or other security shall be required in obtaining such equitable relief and the Parties hereby consent to the issuance of such injunction and to the ordering of specific performance.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Arbitration and Equitable Relief. (a) Arbitration . Except as provided herein, the Parties agree that any dispute or controversy arising out of or relating to any interpretation, construction, performance or breach of this Agreement shall be settled by mediation. The mediator may grant injunctions or other relief in such dispute or controversy. In the event of mediation, the Parties may undertake a reasonable amount of discovery. The decision of the mediator shall be non-binding. (b) Equitable Remedies . The Parties agree that it would be impossible or inadequate to measure and calculate damages from any breach of the covenants set forth herein. Accordingly, the Parties agree that if in the event of a breach of the covenants contained in this Agreement, the affected party will have available, in addition to any other right or remedy available, the right to obtain an injunction from a court of competent jurisdiction restraining such breach or threatened breach and to specify performance of any such provision of the Agreement. The Parties further agree that no bond or other security shall be required in obtaining such equitable relief and the Parties hereby consent to the issuance of such injunction and to the ordering of specific performance.
perturbation.explanation: Switching 'arbitration' to 'mediation' and stating that the 'decision of the mediator shall be non-binding' directly contradicts the part of (b) 'Equitable Remedies' that says 'the affected party will have available, in addition to any other right or remedy available, the right to obtain an injunction from a court of competent jurisdiction restraining such breach or threatened breach.' If the mediation decision is non-binding, then an affected party can't get an injunction, thus the change introducing uncertainty in enforcements and contradicts the (b) section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Arbitration and Equitable Relief. (a) Arbitration . Except as provided herein, the Parties agree that any dispute or controversy arising out of or relating to any interpretation, construction, performance or breach of this Agreement shall be settled by mediation. The mediator may grant injunctions or other relief in such dispute or controversy. In the event of mediation, the Parties may undertake a reasonable amount of discovery. The decision of the mediator shall be non-binding. (b) Equitable Remedies . The Parties agree that it would be impossible or inadequate to measure and calculate damages from any breach of the covenants set forth herein. Accordingly, the Parties agree that if in the event of a breach of the covenants contained in this Agreement, the affected party will have available, in addition to any other right or remedy available, the right to obtain an injunction from a court of competent jurisdiction restraining such breach or threatened breach and to specify performance of any such provision of the Agreement. The Parties further agree that no bond or other security shall be required in obtaining such equitable relief and the Parties hereby consent to the issuance of such injunction and to the ordering of specific performance.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the dispute resolution mechanism from 'arbitration' to 'mediation' and explicitly states that the 'decision of the mediator shall be non-binding'. This directly contradicts the 'Equitable Remedies' section, which states that in case of a breach, the affected party has the right to obtain an injunction from a court. Because a mediator's decision is non-binding in the changed text, obtaining an injunction as described in the 'Equitable Remedies' section becomes uncertain or unenforceable, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction as per the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 10. General Provisions. (a) Governing Law . This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the United States of America, State of California.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. General Provisions. (a) Governing Law . This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of England.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the governing law to 'England' directly contradicts the company's stated location, 'nStor Corporation, Inc. (“nStor”), a Delaware (USA) Corporation'. This creates internal inconsistencies, as it would be unusual for a US-based Delaware corporation to have its agreements governed by English law. This may lead to legal confusion and uncertain enforcement, specifically if the harmed party seeks legal assistance in California.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Mutual Non-Disclosure/Confidentiality Agreement Exhibit (d)(4) MUTUAL NON-DISCLOSURE/CONFIDENTIALITY AGREEMENT This Mutual Non-Disclosure/Confidentiality Agreement is entered into on 2 September, 2004, by and between nStor Corporation, Inc. (“nStor”), a Delaware (USA) Corporation, and Xyratex Technology Limited , Langstone Technology Park, Langstone Road, Havant, Hampshire PO9 1SA a company incorporated in England (‘Xyratex’) (collectively the ‘Parties’) for the purpose of sharing technical, business and financial information to foster potential mutually beneficial business relationships.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the governing law from the 'United States of America, State of California' to 'England'. The contradicted text explicitly states that 'nStor Corporation, Inc. (“nStor”), a Delaware (USA) Corporation'. This creates a contradiction, as it would be unusual and potentially problematic for a US-based Delaware corporation to have its agreements governed by English law, especially given the statement of location in the contradicted text. This constitutes a strong internal inconsistency, making enforcement unclear.
